questions.q: A particle is moving in a straight line with a uniform velocity of 5 ms^-1. What is its acceleration?
The position of an object as a function of time is given by x = 2t^2 - 4t + 5. Find the velocity of the object at t = 2 s.
An object is moving with an initial velocity of 20 m/s. If the acceleration produced is 2 m/s^2, find the velocity after 5 s.
A toy car is moving on a straight track with a uniform acceleration of 2 m/s^2. If its initial velocity is 10 m/s, find the distance covered by it in 4 s.
A particle is moving along a straight line with a velocity of 5 m/s. If its velocity is increased by 10% in 2 s, find its new velocity.
A body is moving with an initial velocity of 20 m/s. If the acceleration produced is 3 m/s^2, find the time taken by the body to cover a distance of 100 m.
An object is moving with a uniform acceleration of 5 m/s^2. If its initial velocity is 10 m/s, find the distance covered by it in 2 s.
A particle is moving along a straight line with a uniform velocity of 2 m/s. Find the distance covered by it in 5 s.
A body is moving with an initial velocity of 5 m/s. If its velocity is increased by 20% in 2 s, find its new velocity.
An object is moving with a uniform acceleration of 4 m/s^2. If its initial velocity is 15 m/s, find the time taken by the object to cover a distance of 100 m.
